articles.title: US and Iran exchange strikes after two US soldiers killed in Jordan
articles.summary: US forces target Iranian sites for the eighth night in a row, while Iran says it fired drones at the US military in Kuwait.

articles.title: Tate brothers arrested in US as further UK charges take total to 59
articles.summary: The Crown Prosecution Service says it has requested their extradition from the US, while their lawyer says they are innocent.

articles.title: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Kyiv
articles.summary: One death is reported in what President Zelensky calls "one of the most massive ballistic attacks" on Ukraine's capital.

articles.summary: He was arrested in 2021 during Cuba's largest anti-government protests in decades and spent five years ⁠in prison.

articles.title: New EU border system tripling time at passport control, airport boss says
articles.summary: Ryanair has also warned passengers travelling to Europe this summer to prepare for extended waits.

articles.title: Ferry carrying 116 passengers sinks off Guyana coast, authorities say
articles.summary: The vessel capsized between Georgetown and Port Kaituma, with 53 people so far rescued.

articles.title: Hungary's president agrees to stand down after parliament backs removal
articles.summary: Tamás Sulyok, seen as an Orbán loyalist, had until Saturday night to agree to end his term.

articles.title: German politician resigns over surrogacy child controversy
articles.summary: Jens Spahn faced criticism over his use of a surrogate, a ban on which he previously backed.

articles.title: Space start-up launches India’s first commercial rocket designed for orbit
articles.summary: India's first space-tech unicorn Skyroot Aerospace successfully launched Vikram-1 on Saturday.

articles.title: Laos says it can't determine cause of tourist deaths linked to methanol
articles.summary: Officials say they have no evidence of poisoning over the 2024 deaths because autopsies were not conducted.
